note: Capstone head-to-head, same run. 009 baseline (tile=1, full, C=1000) vs best 2026 config. Tiling lifts batch QPS +76% at IDENTICAL recall (485->851 @ 0.993); best-recall config reaches 0.9975 @ 730 (+51% QPS AND higher recall than baseline). Latency p50 unchanged by tiling (batch lever). Summary of the 012-020 perf effort below.

levers.verdict: negative: popcount autovectorizes (VPOPCNTDQ/CNT); manual split ~2.2x slower; reverted
latency knob: -14% single-query, -6% batch QPS; heap default, count for serving
recall<->QPS knob: 512-bit ~2x scan QPS; bandwidth-bound confirmed
memory only (4x smaller store); no QPS gain, -1.5 recall pts; rerank not bottleneck
BIGGEST WIN: +73% QPS, identical recall (amortizes base bandwidth)
binary+rerank 462 QPS @ 17ms vs f32 9.3 QPS @ 858ms (~50x end-to-end)
tiling universal, prefix only below ~0.97; best 0.9975@731 / 0.993@850
+36% (768) / +57% (512) serving QPS under load, p50 17->11ms, trade recall
